Name: CDNER
Paper_Title: Cross-Dialectal Named Entity Recognition in Arabic
Venue_Name: The First Arabic Natural Language Processing Conference
Abstract: Arabic language models have recently driven significant advancements in Arabic NER. We study the transferability of Named Entity Recognition (NER) models between Arabic dialects. Resources are unequally distributed: MSA is rich, while other dialects lack datasets. We construct four datasets: MSA from ACE 2005, and manually annotated datasets for Egyptian, Moroccan, and Syrian. We train a span-based NER model on MSA data and test zero-shot performance on dialects using multiple PLM encoders. Annotations and models are publicly available. Our goal is bridging the linguistic gap between MSA and dialects, exploring knowledge transfer in NER. Contributions: 1) A manually annotated NER dataset for Moroccan, Egyptian, and Syrian dialects for evaluation. 2) An efficient span-based NER model trained on MSA data analyzing transferability to other dialects.
